issue_title: Postmortem: emails not sent on 2026-05-19 — investigate recent changes
issue_body:
## Incident summary

No recap emails were delivered to subscribers on **2026-05-19**. Root cause was a regression introduced by PR #172 (batching refactor, `8a72470`) and confirmed during investigation on 2026-05-19. Fixed in PR #179, merged and deployed same day. Missed emails recovered via admin force-run.

## Root cause

The batching refactor (`8a72470`, "Batch Supabase + MLB API calls to stay under subrequest cap") changed the `mlb_game_cache` hit check from a per-game `.select().single()` to a batch `.in()` query. The old code only treated a cache row as a hit when `highlight_url` was truthy:

```js
// Before
if (cached?.highlight_url) { /* use cache */ }
// else: fell through to fetchGameContent
```

The new code used `Map.has()`, which returns `true` even when the stored value is `null`:

```js
// After (buggy)
if (cachedHighlightByPk.has(gamePk)) {
  url = cachedHighlightByPk.get(gamePk); // url = null
}
// url is null → game silently skipped
```

The cron upserts a cache row for every final game it checks, including when no highlight is found yet (highlights aren't posted immediately after a game ends). Once a null-URL row was in the cache, every subsequent tick treated it as a permanent hit, set `url = null`, and skipped the game. The MLB content fetch that would have eventually found the highlight never ran again.

## Timeline

| Time (ET) | Event |
|-----------|-------|
| 2026-05-18 ~10am | PR #172 (batching refactor) merged and deployed |
| 2026-05-18 evening | Games finish; early cron ticks cache games with `highlight_url = null` (highlights not yet posted) |
| 2026-05-18 night | Highlights become available on MLB API, but all subsequent ticks skip games due to null-URL cache bug — no emails sent |
| 2026-05-19 morning | Missed sends detected |
| 2026-05-19 | Investigation opened (#178); root cause identified in `lib/cron-jobs.js:262` |
| 2026-05-19 | Fix committed (one-character: `.has()` → `.get()`), three regression tests added (`lib/cron-jobs.test.js`), PR #179 opened and merged |
| 2026-05-19 | Admin "Run main cron now" force-run executed; missed emails delivered successfully |

## Resolution

**Fix** (`lib/cron-jobs.js:262`): replaced `cachedHighlightByPk.has(gamePk)` with `cachedHighlightByPk.get(gamePk)` so a null-URL cache entry falls through to a fresh `fetchGameContent` call, matching the pre-refactor behaviour. PR #179.

**Recovery**: force-run via `/admin` → "Run main cron now" button. The regular `*/15` cron ticks correctly issue `skipped_no_wake` heartbeats outside the game polling window — a force-run is required to catch up on emails missed during a window that has already passed.

## Why existing tests didn't catch this

The unit tests mocked `fetchGameContent` and `mlb_game_cache` at the function/table boundary. None exercised the full fan-out path with a pre-populated null-URL cache entry. The regression tests added in #179 (`lib/cron-jobs.test.js`) directly cover this scenario.

## Follow-up

- **#180** — End-to-end dry-run testing environment: a `dryRun` flag on `runMainCron` and a `/admin` dry-run button would allow safe pre-deploy verification of the full cron pipeline without sending real emails.
- Consider a catch-up mechanism: automatic detection of games that are Final + have a highlight URL + have no sent notification, even outside the polling window, so a missed window self-heals without requiring a manual force-run.
